fact: October 8 is exactly 281 days into the year, which means there are only 84 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now, hiding up to 10,000 acorns each to survive winter!
The sun is setting almost 2 minutes earlier each day around October 8 in most places, making bedtime feel like it comes super fast.
October 8 falls smack in the middle of Libra season, when the zodiac scales are perfectly balanced - just like how day and night were almost equal two weeks ago!
Maple trees are pumping out their brightest red and orange colors right now because they're basically starving their leaves of green chlorophyll on purpose.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, October 8 means spring is in full swing and baby animals are being born everywhere!
The morning dew gets thicker and sticks around longer on October 8 because the air can't hold as much water when it's getting colder.
Monarch butterflies are in the middle of their epic 3,000-mile journey to Mexico right now, using the sun as their GPS system!
October 8 is exactly 3 weeks and 4 days after the autumn equinox, when Earth was perfectly tilted so day and night were the same length.
Bears in cold places are eating up to 20,000 calories per day around October 8 - that's like eating 40 hamburgers every single day!
The moon looks extra bright and crisp in early October because the cold, dry air acts like a giant magnifying glass in the sky.
Deciduous trees are basically going into hibernation mode right now, shutting down their food factories and getting ready to sleep all winter long.
